sections.paragraph:
εὐρακύλων, ωνος, ὁ (CIL VIII, 26652 ‘euroaquilo’; a hybrid formation of Lat.-Gk. sailor’s language, made fr. εὖρος and Lat. aquilo, B-D-F §5, 1d; 115, 1; Rob. 166) the northeast wind, Euraquilo, the northeaster Ac 27:14 (v.l. εὐροκλύδων, q.v.). JSmith, The Voyage and Shipwreck of St. Paul4 1880, 119ff; 287ff; Hemer, Acts 141f; Warnecke, Romfahrt 37–39.—M-M.

CIL CIL = Corpus Inscriptionum Latinarum—List 3

Lat. Lat. = Latin

Gk. Gk. = Greek

  1. fr. = from

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

Hemer, Acts Hemer, Acts = CH., The Book of Acts in the Setting of Hellenistic History—List 6

Warnecke, Warnecke, Romfahrt = HW., Die tatsächliche Romfahrt des Apostels Paulus—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 411
